commonCitations.medical_records_fees_hospital: N.Y. Pub. Health Law § 18(2)(e) (up to $0.75 per page plus postage; HITECH preemption may apply for patient-directed requests)
commonCitations.medical_records_fees_physician: N.Y. Pub. Health Law § 18(2)(e) (up to $0.75 per page plus postage; same cap applies to physician records)
commonCitations.minor_settlement_approval: N.Y. C.P.L.R. § 1207 (court approval required for settlement of infant/incapacitated-person claims)
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_notice: N.Y. Gen. Mun. Law § 50-e (municipalities); N.Y. Ct. Cl. Act § 10 (State of New York)
commonCitations.tort_claims_act_deadline_days: 90 (Gen. Mun. Law § 50-e for municipal defendants; Ct. Cl. Act § 10(3) — 90 days to file notice of intent against the State)
commonCitations.contingency_fee_rule: 22 N.Y.C.R.R. § 603.25 / § 691.20 (PI fees — 1/3 standard, subject to court review); N.Y. Judiciary Law § 474-a (medical malpractice sliding scale)
commonCitations.certificate_of_merit_medmal: N.Y. C.P.L.R. § 3012-a (Certificate of Merit required in medical/dental/podiatric malpractice actions)
commonCitations.damages_cap_medmal_noneconomic: No statutory cap (New York has not enacted noneconomic damages caps for medical malpractice)
commonCitations.comparative_negligence_standard: N.Y. C.P.L.R. §§ 1411–1413 (pure comparative negligence — plaintiff's recovery reduced by percentage of fault; no bar)
commonCitations.threshold_serious_injury: N.Y. Ins. Law § 5102(d) (nine enumerated categories for no-fault auto threshold — death, dismemberment, significant disfigurement, fracture, permanent loss of use, etc.)
